proof_sketch: The mutation removes a __syncthreads() after the max reduction loop. The reduction tree involves cross-warp dependencies: in iteration s=128, threads 0-127 write to shared_max[0..127]; in iteration s=64, threads 0-63 read shared_max[64..127] written by different warps. Without the barrier, a thread may read a stale value. After the last iteration (s=1), only thread 0 writes shared_max[0]; other threads may read it before that write is visible, causing an incorrect row_max. This race is theoretically reachable with 256 threads (8 warps) and can produce different softmax outputs.
